prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "弓步抬腿" (lunge with leg lift) exercise, side view, facing right. Person posture: Athletic person at the top of a lunge with knee lift: standing on the right leg with right knee almost fully extended (slight bend); left knee driven up high in front of the body to hip height, thigh parallel to floor, shin perpendicular; torso upright; arms in running-style opposition; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: gluteus maximus and quadriceps of the supporting (right) leg, plus hip flexors (psoas) of the lifted (left) leg. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: core muscles and gluteus medius. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "臀大肌 / 股四头肌" pointing to the glute and quad of the supporting leg; "髂腰肌" pointing to the hip flexors of the lifted leg; "核心 / 臀中肌" pointing to the core and gluteus medius area. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
